caption: Attestation
name: attestation
description: A cryptographic attestation that binds an event record to a verifiable identity and, optionally, to a tamper-evident sequence. Named distinctly from the evidences object, which collects detection artifacts: an attestation is about non-repudiation of the record itself — proof it was produced by the identity it claims and was not altered. The signature is computed over a canonical serialization of the event, which includes the acting ai_agent and the hashes of its operating charter, so the attestation also captures the charter fingerprint in force at the time of action. Algorithm-agnostic: classical, post-quantum (ML-DSA), and hybrid signatures are all expressible via the digital_signature object without a schema change.

signature.description: The digital signature over the canonical serialization of the event record, bound to the acting agent's verifiable identity. The signing algorithm, certificate or public-key material, and signing time are carried within the digital_signature object.

entry_hash.caption: Entry Hash
entry_hash.description: Fingerprint of this record's canonical serialization. Linked to by the next entry's prev_entry_hash to form a tamper-evident chain.

prev_entry_hash.caption: Previous Entry Hash
prev_entry_hash.description: Fingerprint of the prior record in the agent's forensic chain, providing tamper-evident ordering. Absent on the genesis entry of a chain.

chain_uid.caption: Chain ID
chain_uid.description: Identifier of the forensic chain or append-only log this record belongs to. Lets an independent verifier locate and validate the chain without trusting the producer's infrastructure.
